name: Virgin of the Tower Loop
name_original: Virgen de la Torre — circular desde La Victoria
description: Hike the easy 4.6-mile Virgen de la Torre circular route from La Victoria, enjoying panoramic views of Mallorca's bays and the Formentor pen
region_intro: Hiking trails around La Victoria traverse a landscape characterized by gentle elevations and open countryside. The region features a mix of agricultural lands and rural paths, with some routes leading to local landmarks like the Virgen de la Torre. These trails offer opportunities to explore the area's natural and cultivated environments. The terrain is generally accessible, making it suitable for various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around La Victoria The most popular hiking route is Virgin of the Tower Loop, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours to complete. This easy loop offers views of the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ruta de Los Alemanes — circular desde San Sebastián de los Ballesteros, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. It traverses rural landscapes and agricultural areas. Local hikers also love the Ascent to the Virgen de la Torre — circular from La Carlota, a 10.9 miles (17.5 km) trail leading through open fields and towards a local landmark, often completed in about 4 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around La Victoria is defined by open fields, rural paths, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in La Victoria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.3 stars from 7 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore La Victoria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Victoria?
A: La Victoria offers a selection of 14 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 4 easy, 9 moderate, and 1 difficult option, ensuring there's a suitable path for most hikers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around La Victoria?
A: Hiking trails around La Victoria feature gentle elevations and open countryside. You'll traverse a mix of agricultural lands and rural paths, with some routes leading to local landmarks. The terrain is generally accessible, making it suitable for various hiking abilities.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in La Victoria?
A: Yes, La Victoria has 4 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1247617936/virgin-of-the-tower-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgin of the Tower Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.6 miles (7.4 km) long and offers pleasant views of the surrounding countryside.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Victoria?
A: The trails in La Victoria are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.3 stars from 7 reviews. Hikers often appreciate the accessible terrain and the opportunities to explore both natural and cultivated environments.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes in La Victoria?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, La Victoria offers one difficult trail. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996313658/la-carlota-and-la-guijarrosa-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Carlota and La Guijarrosa Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is a 12.4-mile (20 km) hike that typically takes over 5 hours to complete, providing a substantial outing through the region's landscapes.

Q: Are there circular walks available in La Victoria?
A: Yes, many of the trails in La Victoria are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Besides the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1247617936/virgin-of-the-tower-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgin of the Tower Loop&lt;/a&gt;, you can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from La Guijarrosa&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.7-mile (7.5 km) route.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see near the hiking trails?
A: While hiking, you can discover several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2884235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalcazar Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; offers a pleasant stop, and you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/924919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water fountain in Guadalcazar&lt;/a&gt;. For a historical touch, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ducal Palace of Fernán Núñez&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: Are the hiking trails in La Victoria suitable for families?
A: Given the generally accessible terrain and the presence of 4 easy trails, many routes in La Victoria are suitable for families. The shorter, less strenuous options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1247617936/virgin-of-the-tower-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgin of the Tower Loop&lt;/a&gt; are ideal for walks with children.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Victoria?
A: The best time to hike in La Victoria is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities, and the landscape is often at its most vibrant.

Q: Are there any trails that offer good viewpoints in La Victoria?
A: Many routes in La Victoria traverse open countryside, providing expansive views of the agricultural lands and rural landscapes. Trails leading towards the Virgen de la Torre, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243732412/ascent-to-the-virgen-de-la-torre-circular-from-la-carlota" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Virgen de la Torre — circular from La Carlota&lt;/a&gt;, often offer elevated perspectives of the area.

Q: Can I find places to rest or picnic along the trails?
A: Yes, you can find designated rest areas near some of the trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2884235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalcazar Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5987278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pineda rest area&lt;/a&gt; are available for hikers to take a break and enjoy a picnic.
tips: Also called the Tower of Don Lucas, it is a tower of Islamic origin, probably from the 12th century. Declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in 1985. In 1924 it became a place of Marian worship, after putting a chapel of the Virgin in the tower.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Virgen de la Torre circular hike?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends at the Ermita de la Victoria. You'll find parking available in this area, which also serves as a convenient base with amenities.

faqs.question: Is the Virgen de la Torre circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, to respect wildlife and other hikers. Remember to carry water for your pet, especially on warmer days.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but the best times are typically spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) when temperatures are milder. Winter hiking is also possible, but summer can be very hot, so an early start is recommended if hiking during those months.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike the Virgen de la Torre circular trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is an easy hiking route, suitable for beginners and hikers of all fitness levels. The paths are generally accessible and not strenuous, making it a great option for a relaxed outdoor experience.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The Virgen de la Torre circular trail is approximately 7.4 kilometers long and typically takes around two hours to complete at a moderate pace.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features easily-accessible paths, with the initial section often being a wide road winding through Mediterranean pine forests. While generally easy, the broader area offers other routes, like the ascent to the Alcudia watchtower, which are more challenging with steeper, sometimes slippery sections. However, this specific circular route remains largely non-strenuous.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to breathtaking panoramic views of the bay of Pollença, the bay of Alcúdia, and the scenic Formentor peninsula. Key viewpoints like the Mirador de la Victoria offer magical settings, especially during sunrise or sunset. On clear days, you might even spot Menorca in the distance.

faqs.question: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail starts and ends at the significant Ermita de la Victoria, a 15th-century hermitage. You'll also pass near the Virgin of the Tower, a historical site. The area also features ancient watchtowers, like the Talaia d'Alcúdia, which were built to guard against pirate attacks.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities available near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area around the Ermita de la Victoria, where the trail begins, provides amenities such as a bar and a restaurant, perfect for a refreshment before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Vereda Blanca, Calle Cataluña, Avenida de Fernán Núñez, Del Camino de Gregorio, and Sendero Cuesta del Lobo.
